title: Kurfürstendamm Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **The Kurfürstendamm is 3.5 km long and starts at Breitscheidplatz**, at the broken Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church, and runs southwest to Rathenauplatz. KaDeWe is not on it: that is Tauentzienstraße, the shorter street leading in from Wittenbergplatz.

**Only the first 1.4 km is worth walking.** Wittenbergplatz to Uhlandstraße, about 18 minutes, holds the Memorial Church, Bikini Berlin, Zoo Berlin, C/O Berlin and the densest run of shops. West of Adenauerplatz the street keeps going and the reasons run out.

**The best things there are free.** Entry to the Memorial Church costs nothing and the memorial hall in the old tower is open Monday to Saturday 10:00 to 18:00. The Bikini Berlin roof terrace, listed daily 9:00 to 21:00, looks straight into the zoo's monkey enclosure.
**C/O Berlin is the paid one worth checking.** The photography museum in the old Amerika Haus is open daily 11:00 to 20:00, 15 euros full and 8 euros reduced, free under 18.
**Savignyplatz is the better evening move.** Four hundred metres north of the boulevard: two leafy squares, restaurants under the S-Bahn arches and bookshops in the railway vaults.

**Getting there takes 15 to 20 minutes from Alexanderplatz**, all inside zone AB. U1 and U9 stop at Kurfürstendamm, U2/U9 and the S-Bahn at Zoologischer Garten, U7 at Adenauerplatz. There is no U-Bahn on the street west of Adenauerplatz.
**It exists because the city was split.** The historic centre stayed in the East, so West Berlin built a downtown here instead, around a bombed church.
